title: Pronunciation Final Exam
learningGoal: The learner will successfully articulate the most phonologically complex structures introduced over the curriculum, including dental fricatives (/θ/, /ð/), labiodental fricatives (/v/), and inter-word catenation.
instructions: This is your final Pronunciation Test! Over the past 3 months, we studied many sounds that do not exist in the Hindi language. Speak clearly into the microphone. Let's see if you remember the 'Th' sound, the 'V' sound, and the rules of fast speaking (Word Glue)! You must score 80% to pass.

contentItems.text: Speak loudly and clearly. Don't rush!

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Bite your tongue slightly for the soft 'Th'. Your teeth must touch your lip for the 'V'.
Make your lips round like an 'O' for Water. Make your teeth touch your lip for Vegetables. Do NOT mix up W and V!
Vibrating 'Th' sound for The and Wea-ther. Z-sound for 'is'.
The 'L' in walked is silent! And the 'ED' sounds like a 'T'. Walk-t.
Remember Word Glue! Fast English speakers combine 'Did' and 'You' to sound like 'Did-joo'.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying every word perfectly separated like a robot.

targetPhrasesOrWords.targetText: Turn it off and pick it up.
targetPhrasesOrWords.transliteration: टर्न इट ऑफ एंड पिक इट अप।
targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: इसे बंद करो और इसे उठाओ।
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: More Word Glue! Tur-Ni-Toff and Pi-Ki-Tup.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: मुझे बहुत भयंकर पेट दर्द है।

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 'K' is completely silent! And the 'is' uses a Z-sound.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: माफ़ करें, मैं खो गया हूँ।
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Polite intonation! 'Excuse me' needs a rising, high-pitch tune.
Emergency intonation! Loud, serious, flat voices. Blast the 'H' in Help.

expectedPronunciationText: Thank you very much Water and vegetables The weather is hot I walked to work Did you see it Turn it off and pick it up I have a terrible stomachache Knowledge is important Excuse me I am lost Help Call an ambulance

feedbackRules.onPass: Astounding! You have completely transformed the way you speak! You mastered changing 'V' sounds, 'W' sounds, silent letters, and fast English word glue. You speak beautifully!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep practicing! Don't let your old habits come back! Make sure you are biting your tongue for the 'Th' sounds, and making your lips round for the 'W' sounds.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Great effort! Watch out for the 'ED' words. 'Walked' sounds like 'Walkt', not 'Walk-ed'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: thank you very much water and vegetables the weather is hot i walked to work did you see it turn it off and pick it up i have a terrible stomachache knowledge is important excuse me i am lost help call an ambulance
acceptedVariants: thank you very much water and vegetables the weather is hot i walked to work did you see it turn it off and pick it up i have a terrible stomach ache knowledge is important excuse me i'm lost help call an ambulance
adminCorrectAnswerSet.phoneticApproximation: Ultimate exam evaluating the suppression of L1 interference (/v/-/w/ merger, dental alveolar shifts, lack of catenation) across highly concentrated target sentences.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Thank you very much. Water and vegetables...

hintText: Speak very confidently. Exaggerate the mouth movements for V and W.
commonMistakes: Reverting to L1 phonetics rapidly due to the high density of stress-tests in a single module.
